channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Noem taps new ICE leaders and moves to identify leakers
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Sunday announced new leadership at the agency tasked with immigration enforcement as she also pledged to step up lie detector tests on employees to identify those who may be leaking information about operations to the media.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israel cuts off electricity supply to Gaza, affecting a desalination plant producing drinking water
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel cut off the electricity supply to Gaza, officials said Sunday, affecting a desalination plant producing drinking water for part of the arid territory. Hamas called it part of Israel's “starvation policy.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump isn't alone in his geopolitical aspirations. Some in Illinois and Oregon want change too
item.description: As President Donald Trump floats grand ideas like reacquiring the Panama Canal and taking over Greenland, some rural residents of Illinois and Oregon are promoting geopolitical change of their own: They want to break away from their states, and perhaps unite with Indiana and Idaho.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why these 2 states didn't 'spring forward' to daylight saving time this morning
item.description: You may have been groggy this morning thanks to our clocks “springing forward” an hour overnight. Unless, of course, you live in either of the two states that don’t observe daylight saving time.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CDC announces investigation into link between autism, vaccines
item.description: The CDC conducted previous investigations, confirming past scientific studies that found no link between the two.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: ICE arrests Palestinian activist who helped lead Columbia University protests, his lawyer says
item.description: A prominent Palestinian activist who helped lead last spring's protests at Columbia University has been arrested by federal immigration agents.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Do you need fluoride after a teeth cleaning? Dental experts weigh in
item.description: Dentists and hygienists often propose fluoride treatments to patients in the dental chair to prevent cavities and strengthen teeth. But the relatively simple procedure – which involves applying a varnish, gel or foam directly to the teeth – isn’t always covered by insurance, especially for adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York governor declares state of emergency for wind-driven brush fire on Long Island
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fast-moving brush fires fanned by high winds burned through a large swath of land on New York's Long Island on Saturday, spewing thick gray smoke into the sky and prompting the evacuation of a military base and the closure of a major highway. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Brush fires on Long Island prompt Governor Hochul to issue State of Emergency
item.description: LONG ISLAND (PIX11) – Governor Kathy Hochul issued a state of emergency as Suffolk County battled multiple brush fires reported in Southampton on Long Island Saturday. Over 70 fire departments and 20 EMS agencies responded to help extinguish the fire.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Magee Hickey, NewsNation

item.title: International Women's Day protests demand equal rights and an end to discrimination, sexual violence
item.description: Women took to the streets of cities across Europe, Africa, South America and elsewhere to mark International Women’s Day with demands for ending inequality and gender-based violence.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge awards nearly $2M after concluding FBI agent was negligent in shooting kidnapped Texas man
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge has awarded nearly $2 million in damages as part of a civil lawsuit after concluding an FBI agent was negligent when he fatally shot a kidnapped Texas man during a botched rescue attempt in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio couple charged after hundreds of living and dead rats found inside home
item.description: A Stark County couple is facing animal cruelty charges after authorities discovered horrific conditions inside their home, which included hundreds of living and dead rats.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, ABC 4

item.title: A total lunar eclipse and blood moon will be visible in March: How and when to watch
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow during a total lunar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Why Chi-Chi’s restaurants are returning 2 decades after their closure
item.description: While multiple casual dining chains are looking to reduce their footprints this year, a once-beloved but long-shuttered brand is preparing for a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas bill could charge transgender people with 'gender identity fraud', if passed
item.description: A bill filed in the Texas House of Representatives this week threatens to charge transgender Texans with a crime for identifying with a gender that differs from their sex assigned at birth.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, KRON4

item.title: Total lunar eclipse, blood moon will be visible in March
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Man fatally shoots brother to save elderly mom in Los Angeles, police say
item.description: “He had no choice but to kill his own brother," a detective with the Los Angeles Police Department said.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of Florida tourist discovered in Georgia woods two years after disappearance: FBI
item.description: The remains of a United Kingdom man missing for nearly two-and-a-half years have been found in a privately owned wooded area in Georgia, the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger asks Idaho judge to block ‘bushy' brows evidence, suggests witness’s artwork skews memory
item.description: Bryan Kohberger asked a Boise judge to block a witness in the Idaho student murders case from testifying about seeing an intruder with "bushy eyebrows" the night of the killings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nikola Jokic makes NBA history with 30-20-20 triple-double in Nuggets' OT win over Suns
item.description: The three-time NBA MVP had the league's first 30-20-20 triple-double in the Denver Nuggets' 149-141 overtime victory over the Phoenix Suns on Friday night. Jokic had 31 points, 21 rebounds and a career-high 22 assists.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KELLY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: A's announce 3-year Las Vegas sponsorship as club looks toward future move
item.description: The Athletics will play in Northern California for at least the next three seasons, but will also represent their future home in Las Vegas and brought four of their star players to drive home that point.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CDC launches 'conflicts of interest' page about vaccine advisory panel
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) published a page on Friday listing the conflicts of interest reported by members of a key vaccine advisory committee.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4

item.title: Police: Colorado woman with 7 kids pointed gun at another driver in road rage incident
item.description: A woman was arrested after she allegedly pointed a gun at another driver in Commerce City on Thursday while seven kids were in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Williams, KRON4

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Teacher found dead at Tennessee high school
item.description: "Our thoughts and prayers are with the family, friends, students, and faculty affected by this heartbreaking event," police said.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, NewsNation

item.title: Family on cruise ship gets lost luggage back after weeks of waiting, but half of their personal items are missing
item.description: Frustrations continue for Jason and Tammy Merritt after waiting a month for their lost luggage, which ended up being on-board the Carnival Cruise Line the entire time.
item.dc:creator: Mecca Thompson/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump offers pathway to citizenship for South African farmers, families
item.description: President Trump has offered an expedited pathway to U.S. citizenship to South African farmers, calling their treatment in the country "terrible" and citing property laws that impose "unjust racial discrimination" against white Afrikaner farmers.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Monarch butterflies wintering in Mexico rebound this year
item.description: The number of monarch butterflies wintering in the mountains west of Mexico City rebounded this year, doubling the area they covered in 2024 despite the stresses of climate change and habitat loss, experts said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elementary student found unharmed after escaping school
item.description: " I just jumped out of the car and tried to grab him to make sure that there was nobody that actually would hit him," Kohler said.
item.dc:creator: Cheyenne Pagan, NewsNation

item.title: CEO of Jack Daniel's responds after Tennessee whiskey pulled from Canada's shelves
item.description: Lawson Whiting, CEO of Brown-Forman, which makes Jack Daniel's, called the move "frustrating" during an earnings call.
item.dc:creator: Nikki McGee, NewsNation

item.title: War heroes, a bomber and military firsts among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: Photos seemed to be flagged for removal simply because their file included the word ”gay,” including an image of the B-29 aircraft Enola Gay, which dropped the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima, Japan, during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why are clocks set forward in the spring? Thank wars, confusion and a hunger for sunlight
item.description: Once again, most Americans will set their clocks forward by one hour this weekend, losing perhaps a bit of sleep but gaining more glorious sunlight in the evenings as the days warm into summer.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Police identify 2 people who died in Feb. 19 midair collision at southern Arizona airport
item.description: MARANA, Ariz. (AP) — Authorities have publicly identified two people killed two weeks ago in the midair collision of two small planes at a southern Arizona airport. Police say 70-year-old Michael Reinath and 76-year-old Linda Gifford died in the Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
